CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-13-2020 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-09-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-28-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-08-2019 Continued To Sep-25-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-17-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT BLUE BUS TOURS COMPANY, LLC's DEMURRER TO Plaintiff's 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT for damages:

Defendant Blue Bus Tours Company, LLC's demurrer to plaintiff Arya Khosravi's first amended complaint is overruled. There are no actions pending between the same parties "on the same cause of action" as those pled in the FAC because plaintiff's first PAGA action and the class action involve only wage and hour claims, while the causes of action asserted in the FAC are for retaliation and wrongful termination. (Code Civ. Proc. 430.10(c); see Bush v. Superior Court (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1374, 1384 [actions were not on the same cause of action where they did not involve invasion of the same primary right].) Even though the claims in all three actions arise out of Plaintiff's employment by Defendant, the central issues in the FAC-whether Plaintiff was subjected to unlawful retaliation and was wrongfully terminated-are not raised in the other two pending actions, and there is no probability of inconsistent rulings or duplication of efforts. Further, although Plaintiff purports to state a cause of action in the FAC under PAGA for penalties "on behalf of himself only," there is no such thing as an "individual" PAGA claim. "A plaintiff asserting a PAGA claim may not bring the claim simply on his or her own behalf but must bring it as a representative action and include 'other current or former employees.'" (Reyes v. Macy's, Inc. (2011) 202 Cal.App.4th 1119, 1123; see also Kahn v. Dunn-Edwards Corp. (2018) 19 Cal.App.5th 804, 810 n.1 ["[A] PAGA action is only a representative action. 'Permitting pursuit of only individual penalties appears inconsistent with PAGA's objectives.'"].) Because the claims in the FAC assert different primary rights than those in the first PAGA action and the class action, staying the action under the doctrine of priority jurisdiction is not warranted.

order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
